titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Minister in China ( (MacMurray))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in China ( MacMurray )See last paragraph for instructions to repeat to Tokyo as Department’s No. 92.
div.p: 165. Department’s 125, July 1, Noon, and text of Japanese proposal communicated to you by Tokyo July 22.The Japanese proposal referred to is the same as the Japanese draft reply to the Chinese note of June 24, handed to the Secretary by the Japanese Ambassador on July 21, p. 792.
Department has now received British Embassy’s reply to its note, text of which was communicated to you in its No. 148 of July 14, 5 p.m.Telegram No. 148 not printed.

[Here follows the substance of the three notes from the British Chargé, dated July 20, printed on pages 788–791; also the text of the Department’s reply, dated July 23, printed supra, which contains an alternate draft reply to the Chinese note of June 24.]

You will use above quoted text as basis for discussing with your colleagues the texts of replies which will be made to Chinese Government’s note and evidence of the attitude that this Government desires to take. Department does not insist on wording as given but desires opportunity to approve any suggested changes.

Repeat to Tokyo as Department’s No. 92 omitting last paragraph, referring to Tokyo’s No. 131, July 21, 5 p.m. to Department.Not printed. Instruct Tokyo to communicate text of Department’s reply to British Embassy to Japanese Foreign Office for its information.
